# md-feedback

> Structured annotation system for human review of AI-generated implementation plans.

md-feedback lets developers annotate markdown plans with three types before AI agents implement them:
- **Highlight** — reading mark, attention flag (non-blocking)
- **Fix** — must be changed before implementation begins (blocking)
- **Question** — needs clarification before proceeding (non-blocking)

Annotations are stored as HTML comments in the markdown file itself — invisible in rendered output, portable across git commits, readable by any text parser.

AI agents interact with annotations directly via a 27-tool MCP server. No copy-paste, no manual export.

## Use when
- You want a human to review an AI-generated plan before implementation begins
- You want quality gates that prevent implementation until all Fix annotations are resolved
- You need to preserve review context across git commits, branches, and AI sessions
- You want structured before/after diffs showing what an AI proposes to change

## Supported platforms
Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Cline, and 7 other MCP-compatible clients.
Also exports to non-MCP platforms via `export_review` tool.

## Key MCP tools
- `evaluate_gates` — pass/fail gate check before implementing
- `list_annotations` — read all human feedback from a plan file
- `apply_memo` — apply a fix to the document
- `generate_handoff` — preserve context across AI sessions
- `get_review_status` — check annotation counts and gate status

## Install
VS Code extension: `yeominux.md-feedback-vscode` (VS Code Marketplace)
npm package: `npm install -g md-feedback`
MCP server: `npx md-feedback` (Node.js 18+ required)

## Documentation
- Full docs: https://github.com/yeominux/md-feedback
- Protocol spec: https://github.com/yeominux/md-feedback/blob/main/SPEC.md
- Changelog: https://github.com/yeominux/md-feedback/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
